supported.deployment: single Go process behind production TLS termination
browsers: Chrome on the protected Ubuntu runner

known_limitations: Sessions, reviewed drafts, and idempotency state are volatile process memory; restart logs users out and there is no shared-session failover.
TLS termination, secret injection, supervision, persistent Provider storage, backups, and Provider authorization are deployment-owner responsibilities.
Studio is fixed to Provider contract 1.2.0 at the pinned Provider commit and does not negotiate contract versions.
Only Chrome on protected Ubuntu CI is release-certified; Firefox and Safari are not certified for v1.1.0.
Usage and discrepancy pages are observational and are not synchronous quota-enforcement authority.
Audit details are not rendered or exported beyond bounded redacted fields and redacted paths.
Studio exposes no password, token, certificate, raw SQL, workflow payload/result, worker identity, or fencing-token administration.
The native local Provider is non-persistent test infrastructure and is not a production Provider deployment.
The release is source/commit based and makes no reproducible binary or container-image claim.
